search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng14 pigmented lesion photography dermoscopy follow-up
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic lesion-risk scoring and melanoma diagnosis
routine imaging and stage-specific follow-up tables
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The workflow does not calculate a lesion score or infer malignancy.
No stage, imaging, excision, referral, or follow-up interval was generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People undergoing clinician-led specialist review of mapped melanocytic or pigmented lesions; previous melanoma and familial-risk contexts need specialist pathways.
setting_applicability: UAE specialist dermatology and skin-cancer surveillance with trained dermoscopy and photography capability.
UAE_applicability: NICE specialist assessment principles are transferable, but UAE skin-cancer, consent, photography, data-governance, pathology, and referral pathways remain required.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG14 recommendations last updated in 2022 were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NICE NG14 remains the current official melanoma guideline identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact melanoma assessment section supports recording specialist mole or pigmented-lesion review, dermoscopy, baseline photography and interval comparison without diagnosing melanoma or selecting excision.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The follow-up section supports documenting clinician-led full skin examination, reviewed prior specialist records, patient concerns, safety net and an arranged follow-up; it does not generate a surveillance interval or investigation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting concerns, personalized follow-up context, clinician-performed full skin and regional lymph-node examination, dermoscopy, medical photography, and completed specialist follow-up without generating an interval or scan.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact lesion count and sites, lesion morphology, dermoscopic findings, risk history, prior melanoma, family history, sun exposure, skin type, diagnosis, biopsy or excision decision, pathology, referral, and surveillance interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
